name: Jest
what_it_does: Widely adopted JavaScript testing framework by Meta with a built-in assertion library, snapshot testing, comprehensive mock system, and code coverage supporting both JavaScript and TypeScript via transform plugins.
use_cases: Write and run unit and integration tests in JavaScript or TypeScript projects using a battle-tested, zero-config-for-CJS ecosystem
Use snapshot testing to assert that serialized component or data output does not change unexpectedly across code changes
Mock modules, timers, and network calls with Jest's built-in manual mock and auto-mock system
Collect code coverage reports across the entire project using Jest's built-in istanbul integration
Run tests in a jsdom environment to unit test browser-targeted JavaScript without a real browser
not_for: New Vite-based TypeScript projects where Vitest provides faster execution with identical API and no transform configuration overhead
End-to-end browser testing across multiple real browser engines — use Playwright or Cypress for that purpose
Native ESM-first projects where Jest's CommonJS transform layer adds friction and occasional module resolution issues
best_when: Maintaining existing JavaScript or TypeScript projects with established Jest configurations, or in organizations where Jest's ecosystem and tooling integrations are already standardized.
avoid_when: Starting a new Vite or ESM-native project where Vitest offers equivalent API with faster cold starts and no transform configuration overhead.

auth.notes: Local test runner library with no network auth layer

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Jest produces detailed diff output for assertion failures and includes matcher-specific messages; stack traces can be noisy in large test suites but are generally actionable
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Test runs are deterministic and idempotent; jest.resetAllMocks() and jest.restoreAllMocks() ensure clean state between tests when configured globally
known_agent_gotchas: Native ESM support requires experimental --experimental-vm-modules flag or a transform; agents must verify project ESM configuration before generating Jest config
jest.mock() calls are hoisted by babel-jest; their placement in source does not reflect runtime execution order
TypeScript support requires ts-jest or babel-jest transform; type errors do not surface by default unless isolatedModules is false in ts-jest
Snapshot files must be committed to version control; agents that regenerate snapshots indiscriminately will create noisy diffs
jest --coverage can significantly slow test runs; agents running tests in tight loops should separate coverage runs from fast feedback runs

security_score_components.security_notes: Local execution only with no network surface; jsdom environment can execute arbitrary scripts so test inputs from untrusted sources should be treated carefully; avoid real credentials in test fixtures
